Control Objective: Restrict access to regulated storage based on classification and business need.
HIPAA Linkage: Technical safeguards for access and data protection
Evidence: Storage permissions, encryption settings, data classification labels

Control Objective: Require strong authentication for privileged, remote, and vendor access.
Federal Linkage: Federal identity and access control expectation
Evidence: Identity provider policy, privileged account export

Control Objective: Review user access on a defined cadence and remove access when no longer needed.
HIPAA Linkage: Administrative safeguards and workforce access controls

Control Objective: Collect, retain, and review logs for security relevant activity.
Evidence: Log source list, alert evidence, review records

Control Objective: Assess vendors before and after onboarding when they touch restricted data.
HIPAA Linkage: Business associate and third party handling expectations
Federal Linkage: Supply chain and federal data protection
Evidence: Vendor inventory, contracts, security review notes

Control Objective: Classify incidents by data type, obligation, impact, and notification pathway.
Document payment data flow and reduce cardholder data exposure.
HIPAA Linkage: Not primary unless payment data connects to patient records
Federal Linkage: Not primary unless federal payment data exists
Evidence: Data flow diagram, processor attestation, segmentation evidence

Control Objective: Separate, mark, and govern CUI and sensitive PII used in the federal pilot.
HIPAA Linkage: May overlap when CUI includes health data
GLBA Linkage: May overlap when financial data is included
Evidence: CUI procedure, folder permissions, training records

Control Objective: Validate backup restoration for systems that support restricted data.
Evidence: Backup report, restore test record, recovery objectives
